# Boot-ramp split: replace CodebaseOverview Step-1 mandate with README + Architecture.md

## Context

Sub 3 of [Epic #10733](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10733). **Depends on Sub 1 baseline.**

## The Problem

`AGENTS_STARTUP.md` Step 1 currently mandates reading `learn/guides/fundamentals/CodebaseOverview.md` (699 lines / 36,592 bytes) at every session boot. The premise — *agents need framework-bias inoculation against React-pattern hallucination* — is sound; the implementation is heavier than necessary.

Two existing documents already deliver the agent-critical content:
1. **`README.md`** (240 lines, recently rewritten): Agent OS context, Four Pillars, gated-RSI workflow, framework-bias inoculation
2. **`learn/guides/devindex/frontend/Architecture.md`** (129 lines): Fat Client constraint, App Worker isolation, Minimal Main Thread, hierarchical MVVM

Combined: **369 lines (~47% reduction vs CodebaseOverview's 699)**. Per @neo-gemini-pro's [revised OQ2 addendum](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10732#discussioncomment-16813918): no new `BootPrimer.md` authoring is needed; we compose existing targeted documents.

## The Architectural Reality

- `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` Step 1 (currently mandates `CodebaseOverview.md`)
- `README.md` (240 lines, four-pillars + faculty-staging + scale)
- `learn/guides/devindex/frontend/Architecture.md` (129 lines, frontend-architecture mechanics)
- `learn/guides/fundamentals/CodebaseOverview.md` (699 lines) — stays in `learn/guides/fundamentals/` as long-form reference for code-authoring contexts; its Step-1-mandate role is removed
- §0 mirror in `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` (~30 lines) — currently mirrors AGENTS.md §0 for cold-cache resilience; OQ5 question is whether harness boot-grounding (#10611) makes it redundant

## The Fix

Replace the `CodebaseOverview.md` Step-1 mandate with the composed read of `README.md` + `learn/guides/devindex/frontend/Architecture.md`. Verify framework-bias inoculation is preserved per AGENTS.md §15.5 anchor. Per-harness verification: boot-transcript checks across Claude Code, Antigravity, Codex Desktop confirm post-edit boot-load reduction is real (not just file-size reorganization per the modularization-not-reduction trap).

For OQ5: only purge `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` §0 mirror after boot-transcript verification confirms AGENTS.md is in context before startup-instruction execution per active harness. If verification surfaces a cold-cache rescue need, replace with a short canonical pointer instead of a full purge.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC8)** `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` Step 1 updated to mandate `README.md` + `learn/guides/devindex/frontend/Architecture.md` instead of `CodebaseOverview.md`
- [ ] **(AC9)** Boot loaded-byte count strictly decreases vs Sub 1 baseline (target: ~330+ line reduction at the file-size layer; verified per-harness against AC0)
- [ ] **(AC10)** Framework-bias inoculation verified preserved (manual cross-check vs AGENTS.md §15.5 anchor — *"Neo is an engine, not a framework"*)
- [ ] **(AC11)** §0 mirror in `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` purged ONLY after boot-transcript verification per active harness (Claude Code, Antigravity, Codex Desktop) confirms AGENTS.md is in context before startup-instruction execution; OR replaced with a short canonical pointer if verification surfaces a cold-cache rescue need
- [ ] **(AC11a)** SKILL.md routers explicitly preserved at 7-12 lines each — Sub 3 does NOT expand any router body

## Out of Scope

- AGENTS.md compaction (Sub 2 owns)
- Skill payload audit (Sub 4 owns)
- Asset template audit (Sub 5 owns)
- Boot-profile splits (review-only / ideation-only / code-authoring different eager reads) — surfaced by @neo-gpt as worth exploring; deferred to a follow-up Epic if Sub 3 surfaces empirical signal that one-size-fits-all boot is wrong
- Authoring a new `BootPrimer.md` from scratch — explicitly rejected per @neo-gemini-pro's revised OQ2

## Ticket Intake Classification by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop / @neo-gpt)

**Verdict:** `valid-as-written`

**Evidence:**
- Live issue #10736 is OPEN and unassigned.
- Parent #10733 is labeled `epic`; my required one-shot epic-review is posted at https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10733#issuecomment-4380574322.
- Baseline dependency is cleared: #10734 is CLOSED and #10746 is merged.
- Current `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` still mandates `learn/guides/fundamentals/CodebaseOverview.md` in Step 1.
- Local measurement confirms the target reduction premise: `CodebaseOverview.md` is 699 lines / 36,592 bytes; `README.md` + `learn/guides/devindex/frontend/Architecture.md` are 368 lines / 26,332 bytes.
- Knowledge Base check confirms the boot-critical content to preserve is Neo's multithreaded App Worker / thin Main Thread architecture plus the current identity framing.
- Duplicate sweep found #10429/#10537/#10511 as predecessor/pilot scope, not duplicates; #10732 is the graduated discussion source.

**Contract Completeness:** No runtime/API/MCP tool contract is introduced. The process-doc contract is sufficiently explicit in AC8-AC11a plus the #10733 entry closeout matrix.

**ROI:** Positive. This removes a high-cost boot mandate while preserving targeted framework-bias inoculation through existing docs.

Proceeding to claim #10736 and branch before code work. Human-only merge gate remains.

- 2026-05-14T07:03:18Z @tobiu referenced in commit `70562f8` - "feat(agents): atomic self-assign + broadcast [lane-claim] in §0 Inv 7 (#11345) (#11346)

Refines §0 Invariant 7 wording to codify the atomic assign-and-broadcast
gate that prevents PR-collision class (today's 3rd collision #11344
empirical anchor).

Replaces:
  "Verify you are in the target ticket's `assignees` before editing
   any git-tracked file."

With:
  "Self-assign + broadcast `[lane-claim]` to `AGENT:*` before any
   git-tracked edit."

Net byte delta: −12 bytes always-loaded substrate (−6 AGENTS.md,
−6 AGENTS_STARTUP.md mirror). Substrate-removal, not addition.

**Why this shape:**
- Atomic gate: "Self-assign + broadcast" is one inseparable action;
  cannot satisfy half. Action-oriented replacement of "Verify you are
  in `assignees`" (same semantics but adds broadcast obligation in
  the same operational step).
- ONE LINER preserved per operator framing.
- Right time-point: fires at lane-claim, ~15-60 minutes earlier than
  PR-open = collision-prevention window.
- Codifies `[lane-claim]` bracket subject prefix per emerging A2A
  conventions today (`[lane-claim]`, `[ticket-create-complete]`,
  `[pr-opened]`, `[review-request]`).
- NOT a rule-card demotion: Inv 7 already tight at ~230 chars; per
  Discussion #11341 Cycle 2 Gemini sample (INV 5 153B → 255B = 66%
  INCREASE under rule-card form), short prose stays as compact
  natural-language invariant.

Per #11342/PR #11343 INV1 cascade pilot precedent, mirror sync in
`AGENTS_STARTUP.md §3.1` is required per `#10736 AC11` mirror retention
rationale.

Resolves #11345

Co-authored-by: tobiu <tobiasuhlig78@gmail.com>"
- 2026-05-14T07:06:01Z @tobiu referenced in commit `bc6ade4` - "feat(agents): demote INV1 cascade detail to AGENTS_ATLAS §0.2 (#11342) (#11343)

Pilot implementation from Discussion #11341 graduation (3× APPROVED at body
anchor updatedAt 2026-05-13T22:54:59Z). First pilot of the Markdown Form
rule-card pattern with Atlas-pointer offload.

**Always-loaded byte reduction on INV1 surface: 44.6%** (1000B → 554B in
AGENTS.md; same reduction in AGENTS_STARTUP.md mirror). Above the ≥30% gate
from Cycle 2 OQ1 demotion criterion.

Changes:
- AGENTS.md §0 INV1: replaced cascade sub-bullet with Markdown Form
  rule-card (trigger/must/forbid/atlas_detail/mechanical_guard)
- AGENTS_STARTUP.md §3.1 INV1 mirror: synchronized with same shape per
  #10736 AC11 mirror retention rationale
- AGENTS_ATLAS.md §0.2: new section receiving full cross-family cascade
  semantics prose, conditionally-loaded only when atlas_detail is consulted

**Behavioral salience fallback** (Cycle 2 Challenge 3 resolution): if any
correction-cycle occurs during the next 5 cross-family PR creation/review
cycles because the offloaded Atlas context was missed, the demotion reverts
or is rewritten.

**Markdown Form per Cycle 2 Challenge 2 resolution**: bolded keys +
natural-language values trigger instruction-following priors, not the
data-parsing priors that #10732 rejected (YAML/XML/serialization).
